description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages (build, test, deploy) that uses a custom Docker image ($CUSTOM_IMAGE) for most jobs and caches pip packages in .pip-cache/ keyed by pyproject.toml. In the build stage define build:wheel (image $CUSTOM_IMAGE, runs "python -m pip install -U pip build" then "python -m build --wheel --sdist", archives the dist/ directory as an artifact named dist-$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA, always runs), build:macos (macos-15-xcode-16 runner, executes "set -euo pipefail", "python3 -V", "python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip", then "pip install \".[test,optional]\"", may be allowed to fail on schedule or manual), and build:windows (Windows runner, PowerShell before_script installs Python via choco, upgrades pip, creates $env:INSTDIR, then runs "pip install \".[test,optional]\"", also allowed to fail on schedule or manual). In the test stage add style_check (image $CUSTOM_IMAGE, installs pip and flake8, runs flake8 across the repository, runs on every non‑schedule pipeline), documentation (needs build:wheel, installs documentation extras, builds Sphinx docs into public/ with two builds, publishes public/ as an artifact), a test matrix test:py39‑py313 (each extends .test_template with python:$PYTHON_VERSION, installs test dependencies, runs "xdoctest icet" and "coverage run -m pytest --verbose --junitxml=report.xml tests/", generates coverage.xml, htmlcov and JUnit report, stores them as artifacts, allowed to fail on schedule and manual except test:py313 which must pass), and pages_verification (extends .pages_template, needs build:wheel, runs the full documentation and coverage workflow and is manual with allowed failure). In the deploy stage include pages (extends .pages_template, publishes the public/ directory as GitLab Pages, triggers on the master branch or any tag, never on schedule) and pypi (image $CUSTOM_IMAGE, before_script installs pip, build and twine, fetches tags, builds a source distribution and uploads it to PyPI using $PYPI_TOKEN, runs manually on a tag). The pipeline respects the defined cache, artifact expiration times, and the specified rules for each job.
